files_to_change.reason: Writer struct doc comment (lines ~46-51) literally claims Append's fsync-before-return matches engine/catalog's WriteAt+Sync durability idiom. Actual Append implementation (lines 326-364) uses plain sequential file.Write (header, then payload) followed by file.Sync -- no WriteAt, no absolute-offset addressing. Fix corrects the comment text only.

files_confirmed_no_change_needed.reason: Already synced by issue #41 subtask 4.5.4.5 (commit 7e7b97a). Its 'Record header and rotation' section already describes Append as 'writes the header, then the payload, then calls file.Sync() before returning' with no WriteAt/catalog-idiom-match claim. No correction needed here.

Read-only reference check: confirmed these do genuinely use WriteAt+Sync (FileManager.WritePage line 281/284, IDAllocator.Next line 204/207). No change needed -- the catalog side of the original comment's factual claim is correct; only the 'writer.go matches it' claim is wrong.

downstream_consumers: None. Doc comment only; no exported API, type, or behavior changes. No callers of Writer.Append or any other engine/wal symbol are affected.
concurrency_check: Confirmed via issue #52 dispatch note that other in-flight agents (issues #43/#47/#56, #50/#53) touch engine/rpc, api/main.go, agents/query, engine/btree, agents/ingestion, docs/LLD/{btree,query-agent}.md -- none touch engine/wal/. No file-level collision risk.
